criteria.criterion: parse: run returns canned scc JSON for files at 100 & 850 LOC -> analyze returns AnalyzerResult with loc['files'] keyed by repo-relative POSIX paths and loc['max_loc'] == 850. PROVE: pytest -q tests/unit/metrics/test_scc_loc.py::test_parse
missing binary: run raises FileNotFoundError -> Unavailable(reason, _ACCRUING_SINCE), no crash, no zeros. PROVE: pytest -q tests/unit/metrics/test_scc_loc.py::test_missing_binary
non-zero exit AND unparseable/empty stdout each -> Unavailable. PROVE: pytest -q tests/unit/metrics/test_scc_loc.py::test_bad_output
successful empty output ([]) -> AnalyzerResult(loc={'files':{},'max_loc':0}), not Unavailable. PROVE: pytest -q tests/unit/metrics/test_scc_loc.py::test_empty_tree
(supplementary) live scc smoke, skipif scc absent. PROVE: pytest -q tests/unit/metrics/test_scc_loc.py::test_live -m integration
ENTRY POINT: scc_loc.analyze(repo_root, scan_roots=None, *, run=_run_scc) -> AnalyzerResult | Unavailable. run is an injectable runner.
LOC PAYLOAD: AnalyzerResult.loc = {'files': {relpath:str: loc:int}, 'max_loc': int}. Flattens two-level scc JSON. POSIX-normalized repo-relative keys. max_loc = max(files.values(), default=0).
FAIL-CLOSED: emits logger.warning('scc unavailable: %s', reason) on all failure paths.
New analyzers live in analyzers/ (NOT adapters/). Package marker src/rebar/metrics/analyzers/__init__.py exists.
scc_loc.py does NOT import from rebar.grounding — stays independent of the grounding layer.
